title: Gradient Drain with Sleep Intervals â€” Sustained 24-Hour Wallet Bleed
narrative: Fraudster maintains persistent access to compromised card over 24-hour window, executing 50-78 transactions with deliberate pacing to avoid instant velocity blocks. Attack begins with test transaction under $5, then escalates to $47-$89 digital goods purchases (ProductCD='W' or 'C') spaced 15-35 minutes apart during business hours (txn_hour 9-17), pausing 6-8 hours overnight (txn_hour 1-6 shows zero activity). Resumes at dawn with renewed burst. Total drain $2,800-$5,400. Fraudster rotates between 3-4 merchant categories (MCC 5816, 5735, 5968) but maintains same device fingerprint and P_emaildomain throughout. Card1_txn_count_24hr climbs steadily from 8 to 50+. Transaction amounts show flat distribution (card1_amt_zscore oscillates -0.3 to +0.8, never spiking). This mimics legitimate user behavior while systematically draining available credit.
indicators: card1_txn_count_24hr exceeds 50 with sustained 15-35 minute inter-transaction intervals
Zero transaction activity during txn_hour 1-6 followed by immediate resumption at txn_hour 7-8
TransactionAmt flat distribution $47-$89 across 80%+ of transactions (card1_amt_zscore remains -0.5 to +1.0)
Consistent P_emaildomain and device fingerprint across all 50+ transactions despite rotating MCC codes
ProductCD restricted to 'W' or 'C' (digital goods) with no physical delivery or addr1 variance
Initial test transaction under $5 followed by immediate escalation to $47+ average within 2 hours
Total 24-hour cumulative spend $2,800-$5,400 with no declines or step-down behavior
summary: A compromised card shows 50+ transactions over 24 hours with deliberate 15-35 minute spacing, a flat $47-$89 amount pattern, and complete silence overnight (1-6am) before resuming at dawn. The same device and email persist across all purchases while merchant categories rotate.
reasoning: The fraudster is pacing transactions to stay under velocity thresholds while draining the card's credit limit, using the overnight pause and consistent transaction amounts to mimic a legitimate user's shopping pattern rather than triggering instant fraud blocks.
indicator_explanations: Card shows methodical pacing with 50-plus hits spread across a full day
Attack pauses completely overnight then resumes precisely at business hours
Transaction amounts cluster in narrow band to stay below velocity triggers
Same email and device used throughout despite changing merchant categories
Only digital goods purchased with no shipping address involvement
Tiny probe transaction immediately followed by sustained higher-value draining
Wallet drained for thousands with zero declines suggesting compromised valid card
